heading: Editorials and other matter as “advertisements”
label: 1734 Editorials and other matter as “advertisements”
text: Whoever, being an editor or publisher, prints in a publication entered as second class mail, editorial or other reading matter for which he has been paid or promised a valuable consideration, without plainly marking the same “advertisement” shall be fined under this title.
notes: Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $500”.Pub. L. 103–322
